success_surface: Row deletion task on spreadsheet requiring identification and removal of blank rows while preserving data integrity and structure
effective_behaviors: Used openpyxl instead of pandas for row-level structural manipulations like deletion
Collected all target row indices before performing deletions to avoid index shifting errors
Iterated through potential deletion rows in reverse order or sorted descending before execution
Implemented comprehensive blank-row detection checking all cells in a row against multiple empty conditions (None, empty string, whitespace-only)
Validated target sheet existence before attempting manipulation
Skipped header row when identifying data rows for deletion
Logged intermediate counts and dimensions for verification of deletion impact
Ensured output directory exists before saving modified workbook
evidence: openpyxl used exclusively for load_workbook, delete_rows, and save operations
rows_to_remove list populated during one pass, deleted in sorted(reverse=True) loop
Detection check: all(cell.value is None or cell.value == '' or str(cell.value).strip() == '' for cell in row_cells)
Explicit sheet name validation: if 'SH' not in wb.sheetnames: raise ValueError
Iteration range: range(max_row, 1, -1) starting from row 2 to skip header
Print statements logged 'Rows to remove', 'New total rows', and summary statistics

memory_items.title: Structural Row Operations Use openpyxl
memory_items.lesson: For tasks involving row insertion, deletion, or sheet restructuring, openpyxl provides reliable native methods that preserve workbook structure better than pandas.
memory_items.generalization: Prefer openpyxl over pandas when manipulating row/column count, merged cells, or when formula/format preservation during structural changes is required.

memory_items.title: Two-Pass Deletion Pattern Prevents Index Errors
memory_items.lesson: Collecting all rows to delete in a first pass, then executing deletions in reverse order prevents cascading index shifts that cause missed or duplicated deletions.
memory_items.generalization: Always separate target discovery from target execution. Store indices first, sort descending, then delete to maintain stable row references throughout modification.

memory_items.title: Comprehensive Empty Cell Detection
memory_items.lesson: Rows may contain None, empty strings, or whitespace-only values; all must be checked to correctly identify truly blank rows.
memory_items.generalization: When detecting empty rows/cells, validate against multiple empty patterns (None, '', stripped whitespace) across all relevant columns, not just the first column.

memory_items.title: Header Row Protection During Structural Changes
memory_items.lesson: Skipping header rows when applying structural modifications preserves the schema and prevents accidental header loss.
memory_items.generalization: Start structural row operations from row 2 onward unless headers are explicitly included in the operation scope.

memory_items.title: Pre-Modification Validation Checklist
memory_items.lesson: Confirming sheet existence, directory paths, and expected ranges before writing prevents runtime failures and lost work.
memory_items.generalization: Validate target sheets exist, output directories are ready, and input file loads successfully before modifying any workbook content.

edits.content:
## Structured Row Deletions

### Blank Row Removal Protocol
- **Detection Phase**: Scan all columns in each row to identify truly blank rows (None, empty string, whitespace-only).
- **Index Collection**: Store all target row numbers in a list before making any modifications.
- **Deletion Order**: Sort collected indices in descending order and delete sequentially to prevent index offset errors.
- **Header Safety**: Begin scanning from row 2 to protect header rows unless instructed otherwise.

### Tool Selection Guidelines
| Operation Type | Recommended Tool | Reason |
|----------------|------------------|--------|
| Data transformation & calculations | pandas | Fast, vectorized operations |
| Row/column insertion/deletion | openpyxl | Preserves workbook structure natively |
| Formula/style preservation | openpyxl | Direct cell object access |
| Structural reorganization | openpyxl | Native sheet manipulation methods |

### Pre-Execution Validation
- Confirm target worksheet exists in workbook before manipulation.
- Ensure output directory exists using os.makedirs with exist_ok=True.
- Load workbook successfully and verify max_row/max_column values match expectations.
- Log initial state (row count, blank row locations) for audit trail.
